segments.content: Welcome back. Tonight we're exploring how the same molecules that can kill you are now being turned into life-saving drugs.
From cone snails to scorpions, venom is one of nature's most sophisticated chemical libraries.
Fact one. Cone snail venom contains more than one hundred distinct peptides, and a single species may produce over fifty thousand unique toxin variants.
Researchers catalog these peptides the way librarians catalog books — each molecule filed by structure, target, and effect.
Fact two. One peptide from cone snail venom, ziconotide, became an FDA-approved pain drug that works when opioids fail.
The venom that kills you is also saving lives — and scientists finally mapped how.
Modern labs use automated screening to test thousands of venom fractions against ion channels in a single afternoon.
If this blend of science and wonder helps you unwind, you're welcome to like and subscribe.
